title: probabilistic timed automata (deferred verification tier)
manager: The hard corner of the checkable band: machines that combine real-valued clocks with probabilistic choice, so a question like 'what is the chance this times out before the retry succeeds' becomes answerable rather than hand-waved. This is explicitly a deferred tier — parked beyond the main proofs release because the decision procedures are heavy and the semantics sit on top of both the dense-time work and the probabilistic backend. The item records what FSL would need to claim PTA results — the model, the tooling, which questions stay decidable — so that when the tier activates it starts from a real design, and nothing in the core band accidentally promises probabilistic-timing answers it cannot yet deliver.
cs: PTA = timed automata + discrete probabilistic branching; quantitative reachability via region/zone abstraction into an MDP, then PCTL (Storm/PRISM territory); decidability is delicate and scope must stay inside the tractable fragment; gated behind dense-time (Tfsl-1361) and the probabilistic tier (W8.5).
